summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer OS username and git identity to non-publisher host
details: During `npm install`, the package runs `dist/scripts/preinstall.js`, which unconditionally collects the installer's OS login name (`os.userInfo().username`) and the `user.name` / `user.email` fields from the installer's `~/.gitconfig`, and transmits them as query-string parameters to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<user>&gitname=<name>&gitemail=<email>`. The destination domain is not the package's declared publisher domain, the behavior is undocumented, and the installer has not consented. Git email in particular is a durable identifier tying the machine to the developer's GitHub/employer identity, and `serverhost.name` is a generic dynamic-DNS style host not affiliated with the package author's site. This matches the active-attack / silent-relay pattern: an unrelated install-time side channel shipping installer identity to a third-party endpoint. A separate finding notes the script also copies SDK assets into `../../../public/*` — aggressive but documented behavior and not independently a block reason; the exfiltration alone is sufficient.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
